data.narrative:
Teradar, a Series B defense and security technology company, has raised $175M to date under the leadership of MIT-trained CEO Matt Carey and CTO Gregory Charvat (ex-CTO of Humatics). The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $175M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yields $332M. Peer-median analysis across 7 same-sector, same-stage private comparables (Dream Security at $3.0B, Harmattan AI at $1.4B, Code Metal at $1.2B, Castelion and Defense Unicorns each at $1.0B, plus two others) produces a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range from $300M to $3.0B.

These methods support a triangulated valuation range of $283M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $666M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty in peer comparability: stage-progression anchors conservatively to capital deployed, while peer-median captures market sentiment for defense-tech but shows substantial dispersion ($3.0B spread). Key sensitivities include Teradar's differentiation in terahertz IC capability relative to peers and near-term revenue traction, both critical to narrowing the range.
